title: Morelosia montana
intro: Morelosia montana, also known as Mountain Morelosia, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Morelosia montana include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Morelosia montana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of Morelosia montana is further detailed within the subfamily Rauvoloideae. The genus Morelosia is closely related to the genus Rauwolfia. Morelosia montana is a rare and lesser-known species.
